text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository (a React date-picker component library) at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: `DatePicker` accepts an explicit `timeZone` prop (an IANA zone name such as "Pacific/Kiritimati") meant to pin the picker to a timezone different from the browser's local timezone. When that `timeZone` differs significantly from the local/browser timezone, the picker gets the calendar day wrong in three places: (1) clicking a day in the calendar grid produces an `onChange` date, and a visually 'selected' day, that are off by one day (in the configured timeZone) from the day the user actually clicked; (2) the formatted value shown in the text input is off by one day because it reflects UTC/browser-local time instead of the configured timeZone; (3) with `selectsMultiple`, the calendar does not consistently highlight the correct set of days as selected in the target timezone. In short, calendar day-highlighting, the input's formatted display value, and multi-date-selection highlighting must all consistently interpret `selected` / `startDate` / `endDate` / `selectedDates` (and the values handed to `onChange`) in terms of the configured `timeZone`, not the browser's local timezone. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: this is a Node/TypeScript project managed with Yarn (Berry) via Corepack; dependencies are already installed (re-run `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable` if you ever need to). Tests use Jest — run a single file with `node_modules/.bin/jest <path>`.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the `timeZone` handling from `DatePicker` through calendar selection, input formatting, and multi-date highlighting, then patch the shared date interpretation points and run focused Jest coverage plus relevant type/lint checks.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!*node_modules*'"' | head -80 && git status --short && rg -n \"timeZone|selectsMultiple|isSelected|selectedDates|startDate|endDate\" src | head -240"

text: The source already has timezone conversion utilities, but the conversions are applied inconsistently: selection converts early to an instant, while rendering and formatting still compare/format several props as browser-local `Date` values. I’m narrowing this to a single “calendar representation” boundary so comparisons use target-zone fields and callbacks retain the intended instant semantics.

text: The fix will stay localized to the library boundary: `DatePicker` will pass timezone-projected selection props into `Calendar`/`Day`, while `DatePicker` input formatting will format the original instants directly in the configured zone. Selection validation and multi-date toggling will compare projected calendar dates before converting the clicked day back for `onChange`.

The core edits are in place: timezone-aware formatting, projected selection props for calendar highlighting, and pre-conversion validation/toggling. I’m now checking range/input paths and compiling, because those paths can reintroduce the same local-vs-target comparison bug even when ordinary day clicks are correct.

text: The focused tests pass and type-checking is clean, but the review exposed one subtle remaining issue in time preservation: converting to an instant before applying the selected time makes the result browser-zone dependent. I’m correcting that ordering so all date/time mutations happen in target-zone calendar space, with one conversion at callback time.

text: The browser-zone simulation now passes as well (`TZ=America/Los_Angeles`), including the existing timezone and multiple-selection suites. Type-checking and ESLint are clean; I’m doing a final formatting/diff review to ensure the patch is limited to source behavior and preserves the user’s unrelated `package.json` changes.

text:
Fixed the timezone handling in `src/`:

- Calendar selection and highlighting now use the configured timezone.
- Input formatting supports timezone-aware single, range, and multiple dates.
- Multi-date toggling compares dates in the target timezone.
- Date/time changes preserve target-zone time fields before converting callbacks to instants.
- Range input parsing now converts dates from the configured timezone.

Verified with type-checking, ESLint, Prettier, and focused Jest suites under both UTC and `TZ=America/Los_Angeles`.
